scenario: SCENARIO
f9: F9 NOTE

scenario: Did INS forget the line item?
f9:
For decades we have vetted, managed, trained and warrantied subcontractors for roofing, gutters, painting, fencing, siding etc. due to storm restoration in the DFW area.

We charge a minimum 20% overhead and profit above the subcontractors cost as we act as the single point of contact for all subcontractors' trade complexities, plus cover all their liability and warranty for our clients.

Project Requires General Contracting
This project involves coordination of multiple trades—including roofing, gutter replacement, exterior painting, window screen repair, and possible stucco or interior restoration. Per industry standards and Xactimate guidelines, O&P is warranted whenever three or more trades are involved and coordinated by a general contractor.

Complex Scheduling and Management
Each trade must be scheduled in the correct sequence, managed for quality and timing, and supervised for code compliance and safety. This requires administrative staff, project managers, and dedicated personnel which all reside at our physical office —all part of our company’s operational infrastructure.

We Are Not a Single-Trade Contractor
Unlike subcontractors, we do not perform only one scope of work. We maintain a physical office, estimators, coordinators, insurance claim specialists, and field supervisors. These are all actual employees of IFC Contracting Solutions— these employees are the overhead which is built into O&P. These costs are necessary to support project execution, client communication, and warranty management.

Insurance Restoration Demands Administrative Oversight
Insurance jobs require extra documentation, invoicing, inspection scheduling, supplemental negotiation, and warranty coordination, all of which involve personnel beyond the jobsite. These employees reside at our physical location and work to adhere to the highest construction standards. This is not reimbursed in trade line items and is accounted for within O&P.

xact_description: Asphalt starter - laminated double layer starter
scenario: Did INS FORGET the line item?
f9:
The insurance report left out the Starter. 

We are requesting X LF of Laminate Double Layer Starter. Please see attached EagleView Report for confirmation of the measurements.

1. Per Xactimate pricing, this costs an additional $XX.xx.

a. This house currently has Laminate Double Layer Starter installed and it cannot be reused.

b. For slate-look shingles, the starter course requires a double layer to match the thickness and durability of the main shingle. This is necessary to ensure that the first row of shingles stays secure, especially given the heavier weight of slate-look shingles compared to traditional asphalt shingles. The starter course needs to cover the exposed edges and provide a strong, secure base for the rest of the roof.

scenario: Did INS FORGET the line item?
f9:

We are requesting XX LF of Starter.

a. Starter is a different material and is installed at a higher rate than field shingles, therefore it is inappropriate to bundle it into the waste and leads to inaccurate material estimates. Please see attached EagleView for measurements.

scenario: Is INS including starter in the waste calculations?
f9:
The insurance report left out the Starter.

We are requesting 535.00 LF of Starter.

1. Per Xactimate pricing, this costs an additional $1,238.73.

a. Starter is a distinct roofing component with a separate material profile and a higher associated labor rate compared to field shingles. As such, it should not be included within waste calculations for field shingles, as doing so results in inaccurate material and labor estimates. A review of the corresponding Xactimate line item details will confirm that starter installation is priced at a higher labor rate, reflecting the specialized nature of its application at roof edges.

b. If this was standard practice to include start in the waste across the industry, then the separate line item in Xactimate would not exist.

c. USAA is the outlier in this regard and is the only Insurance company that makes home owners pay out-of-pocket for starter. Most other Insurance companies pay to replace the starter on a customer's roof separately from the field shingle as the installation and labor associated is distinct from the field shingles in the same way installing ridge shingle is different from a field shingle.

c. Please see attached EagleView for measurements.

xact_description: Add. layer of felt/underlayment, remove & disp.
scenario: Is INS saying they dont include waste on Felt?
f9:
Our line item 7 covers insurance line 7.

We are requesting an additional of 6.16 SQ for Roofing Felt.

1. The difference is $1,062.96. Per Xactimate pricing, the cost is $7,469.86. While in insurance report cost is $6,406.90.

a. Roofing felt, like shingles, must be installed in a staggered and overlapping pattern to ensure proper water shedding and code compliance. During this process, cuts are required at eaves, valleys, hips, ridges, and roof penetrations, which inevitably generates material waste. This waste is a necessary part of achieving a watertight underlayment system. Applying a waste percentage to roofing felt is both customary and essential to complete the work properly and bring the property back to its pre-loss condition.

b. Please see attached EagleView showing the waste calculation of 16% for the shingles. It also show the exact squares for the Felt.

c. Currently the insurance is paying for 174.84 SQ while we are requesting 181 SQ which is an additional 6.16 SQ.

xact_description: Additional charge for steep roof - 7/12 to 9/12 slope
scenario: INS did not include waste calculations?
f9:
Our line item 3 covers insurance line 3

We understand steep charges are calculated per square and separate from waste. However, in this case, the waste is installed on steep slopes and should be included in the total steep charge calculation.

EagleView shows 13% waste due to the roof’s complexity. That waste material is not discarded—it is cut, lifted, and installed on the steep slope, requiring the same labor and safety measures.

The request to increase from 23.31 SQ to 25.18 SQ reflects actual material installed on steep surfaces, not a markup.

"Our line item 17 covers insurance lines 14 and 15.

1. The difference is $48,998.05. Insurance lines 14 and 15 total $20,253.15 while our sub bid totals $69,251.20

scenario: Are you REPLACING a Xactimate item with a bid item?
f9:
Our Line Item X covers Insurance Line Item X,

a. The line item is being used as if this is for an entire standing seam roofing system, but it is not, this is for a custom accent piece that ties into the walls of the home. Accent pieces are always higher in price due to complexity and difficulty when fabricating and installing.

b. The amount of panels that are fabricated and seamed together by hand is significantly higher per SQFT than if you were doing an entire roof, where you can use a different type of panel without as much complexity.

c. Additionally, because the sqft is left-to-right there are more panels, cuts and complexities than if the sqft was entirely top to bottom like on a full standing seam roofing system. Also roof's seam clamped and seamed by tool/machine as is is a mechanically fastened roof

d. Xactimate cannot capture the complexity of this job so we have to go to the market and get a bid.

e. If you would like us to get a second bid for both accent pieces we can, but from experience the subcontractor we are using is the most affordable.

c. Because of the custom design and short distance between the pool and the roof we have to protect the pool or we will not be able to warrant our work against granules penetrating the filter system.
